    charset utf-8;
    
    # Security headers
    add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
    add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
    add_header X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block" always;
    add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" always;
    
    # Max upload size (for JobPost attachments)
    client_max_body_size 10M;
    
    # Static files (collected by Django collectstatic)
    location /static/ {
        alias /app/staticfiles/;
        expires 30d;
        add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
        access_log off;
    }
    
    # Media files (user uploads - JobPost images, Author avatars)
    location /media/ {
        alias /app/media/;
        expires 7d;
        add_header Cache-Control "public";
    }
    
    # Health check endpoint (for Kubernetes probes)
    location /health/ {
        access_log off;
        return 200 "healthy\n";
        add_header Content-Type text/plain;
    }
    
    # Django application
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://django_backend;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        
        # Timeouts
        proxy_connect_timeout 60s;
        proxy_send_timeout 60s;
        proxy_read_timeout 60s;
        
        # Buffering
        proxy_buffering on;
        proxy_buffer_size 128k;
        proxy_buffers 4 256k;
        proxy_busy_buffers_size 256k;
    }
    
    # API endpoints for JobPost CRUD operations
    location /api/ {
        proxy_pass http://django_backend;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        
        # Longer timeout for batch operations on Skills/JobPost
        proxy_read_timeout 120s;
    }
    
    # WebSocket for real-time notifications (if implemented)
    location /ws/ {
        proxy_pass http://django_backend;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_read_timeout 86400;
    }
}

Command:

# Create ConfigMap from Nginx config file
kubectl create configmap nginx-config \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --from-file=django.conf=config/nginx/django.conf

# Or with default key name (filename becomes the key)
kubectl create configmap nginx-config \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --from-file=config/nginx/django.conf

Output:

configmap/nginx-config created

✅ Verification:

# Verify file content is stored
kubectl get configmap nginx-config -n anihpj-production -o jsonpath='{.data.django\.conf}' | head -20

# Check the size of the stored configuration
kubectl get configmap nginx-config -n anihpj-production -o json | jq '.data["django.conf"] | length'

# View how to mount in deployment
cat <<EOF
# Deployment volume mount example:
volumes:
- name: nginx-config
  configMap:
    name: nginx-config
containers:
- name: nginx
  volumeMounts:
  - name: nginx-config
    mountPath: /etc/nginx/conf.d/django.conf
    subPath: django.conf
EOF
# config/nginx/django.conf
# Nginx configuration for anihpj Django jobpost application
# Handles: static files, media files, proxy to Gunicorn

upstream django_backend {
    # Gunicorn workers - will be resolved to pod IPs by Kubernetes DNS
    server 127.0.0.1:8000;
    keepalive 32;
}

server {
    listen 80;
    server_name jobpost.example.com www.jobpost.example.com;
    charset utf-8;
    
    # Security headers
    add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
    add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;

2847
Example 4: Create ConfigMap from Directory

Scenario: Create a ConfigMap containing multiple configuration files from a directory, including Gunicorn, Celery, and logging configurations.

📄 Context - Configuration Directory Structure:

config/gunicorn/
├── gunicorn.conf.py      # Gunicorn workers, threads, binding
├── celery.conf.py        # Celery worker configuration
└── logging.conf          # Python logging configuration

📄 config/gunicorn/gunicorn.conf.py:

# gunicorn.conf.py - Gunicorn configuration for anihpj Django app
import multiprocessing
import os

# Server socket
bind = os.environ.get('GUNICORN_BIND', '0.0.0.0:8000')
backlog = 2048

# Worker processes
workers = int(os.environ.get('GUNICORN_WORKERS', multiprocessing.cpu_count() * 2 + 1))
worker_class = os.environ.get('GUNICORN_WORKER_CLASS', 'sync')
worker_connections = 1000
timeout = int(os.environ.get('GUNICORN_TIMEOUT', 30))
keepalive = 2

# Process naming
proc_name = 'anihpj-jobpost'

# Logging
accesslog = '-'
errorlog = '-'
loglevel = os.environ.get('GUNICORN_LOG_LEVEL', 'info')
access_log_format = '%(h)s %(l)s %(u)s %(t)s "%(r)s" %(s)s %(b)s "%(f)s" "%(a)s" %(D)s'

# Server mechanics
daemon = False
pidfile = None
umask = 0
user = None
group = None
tmp_upload_dir = None

# SSL (handled by Kubernetes Ingress, not Gunicorn)
keyfile = None
certfile = None

📄 config/gunicorn/logging.conf:

[loggers]
keys=root,django,jobpost

[handlers]
keys=console,json

[formatters]
keys=standard,json

[logger_root]
level=INFO
handlers=console

[logger_django]
level=INFO
handlers=console
qualname=django
propagate=0

[logger_jobpost]
level=DEBUG
handlers=json
qualname=jobpost
propagate=0

[handler_console]
class=StreamHandler
level=DEBUG
formatter=standard
args=(sys.stdout,)

[handler_json]
class=StreamHandler
level=DEBUG
formatter=json
args=(sys.stdout,)

[formatter_standard]
format=%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s
datefmt=%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S

[formatter_json]
format={"time": "%(asctime)s", "name": "%(name)s", "level": "%(levelname)s", "message": "%(message)s"}

Command:

# Create ConfigMap from entire directory
kubectl create configmap gunicorn-config \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --from-file=config/gunicorn/

# This creates keys for each file:
# - gunicorn.conf.py
# - celery.conf.py
# - logging.conf

Output:

configmap/gunicorn-config created

✅ Verification:

# List all keys (file names)
kubectl get configmap gunicorn-config -n anihpj-production -o jsonpath='{.data}' | jq 'keys'

# Check specific file content
kubectl get configmap gunicorn-config -n anihpj-production \
  -o jsonpath='{.data.gunicorn\.conf\.py}' | head -15

# View ConfigMap details
kubectl describe configmap gunicorn-config -n anihpj-production
[
  "celery.conf.py",
  "gunicorn.conf.py",
  "logging.conf"
]

# gunicorn.conf.py - Gunicorn configuration for anihpj Django app
import multiprocessing
import os

# Server socket
bind = os.environ.get('GUNICORN_BIND', '0.0.0.0:8000')
backlog = 2048

# Worker processes
workers = int(os.environ.get('GUNICORN_WORKERS', multiprocessing.cpu_count() * 2 + 1))
worker_class = os.environ.get('GUNICORN_WORKER_CLASS', 'sync')

Name:         gunicorn-config
Namespace:    anihpj-production
Labels:       <none>
Annotations:  <none>

Data
====
celery.conf.py:
----
...
gunicorn.conf.py:
----
...
logging.conf:
----
...
Example 5: Create ConfigMap with Hash Suffix for Immutable Deployments

Scenario: Create a ConfigMap with an appended hash suffix to ensure deployments use specific configuration versions, enabling safe rollbacks and GitOps workflows.

📄 Context - Immutable ConfigMap Strategy:

# Why use --append-hash?
#
# Problem: Updating a ConfigMap doesn't automatically restart pods
# Solution: Append hash to ConfigMap name, triggering deployment update
#
# Without --append-hash:
# ConfigMap: django-config (always same name)
# Deployment reference: configMapRef: name: django-config
# Result: Pod doesn't restart when ConfigMap content changes
#
# With --append-hash:
# ConfigMap: django-config-abc123def (name includes content hash)
# Deployment reference: configMapRef: name: django-config-abc123def
# Result: New ConfigMap name triggers deployment rollout
#
# GitOps Flow:
# 1. Generate ConfigMap with hash: kubectl create configmap --append-hash
# 2. Update deployment to reference new ConfigMap name
# 3. Commit both to Git
# 4. Flux/ArgoCD applies changes
# 5. Deployment automatically rolls out with new config

📄 Django Feature Flags Configuration:

# config/features/v2.0.0-features.env
# Feature flags for anihpj jobpost v2.0.0 release

# Skills model features
ENABLE_SKILLS_AUTOCOMPLETE=true
ENABLE_SKILLS_SYNONYMS=true
SKILLS_AUTOCOMPLETE_MIN_CHARS=2

# User model features
ENABLE_USER_PROFILES=true
ENABLE_USER_AVATARS=true
USER_AVATAR_MAX_SIZE_KB=500

# Author model features
ENABLE_AUTHOR_VERIFICATION=true
ENABLE_AUTHOR_BADGES=false
AUTHOR_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED=false

# Location model features
ENABLE_LOCATION_SEARCH=true
ENABLE_LOCATION_AUTOCOMPLETE=true
LOCATION_SEARCH_RADIUS_KM=50

# JobPost model features
ENABLE_JOBPOST_RECOMMENDATIONS=true
ENABLE_JOBPOST_ALERTS=true
ENABLE_JOBPOST_ANALYTICS=true
MAX_JOBPOST_PER_AUTHOR=50
JOBPOST_EXPIRY_DAYS=30

Command:

# Create ConfigMap with hash suffix
kubectl create configmap feature-flags \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --from-env-file=config/features/v2.0.0-features.env \
  --append-hash \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml

# Actually create it
kubectl create configmap feature-flags \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --from-env-file=config/features/v2.0.0-features.env \
  --append-hash

Output:

# Dry-run output shows the hash:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: feature-flags-7c9h8d6m5f
  namespace: anihpj-production
data:
  ENABLE_SKILLS_AUTOCOMPLETE: "true"
  ...

# Actual creation:
configmap/feature-flags-7c9h8d6m5f created

✅ Verification:

# List ConfigMaps matching pattern
kubectl get configmaps -n anihpj-production | grep feature-flags

# Get the exact name for deployment reference
CONFIGMAP_NAME=$(kubectl get configmaps -n anihpj-production -o name | grep feature-flags | tail -1)
echo "Use in deployment: ${CONFIGMAP_NAME#configmap/}"

# Update deployment to use new ConfigMap
kubectl set env deployment/jobpost-web \
  --from=configmap/${CONFIGMAP_NAME#configmap/} \
  -n anihpj-production

# Verify deployment rolled out with new config
kubectl rollout status deployment/jobpost-web -n anihpj-production
feature-flags-7c9h8d6m5f   17    45m

Use in deployment: feature-flags-7c9h8d6m5f

deployment.apps/jobpost-web env updated

deployment "jobpost-web" successfully rolled out
Example 6: Create ConfigMap with Binary File (Base64 Encoded)

Scenario: Create a ConfigMap containing a binary file (SSL certificate bundle or compressed archive) that needs to be mounted in pods.

📄 Context - CA Certificate Bundle for Database SSL:

# Why use binary ConfigMap?
#
# Scenario: PostgreSQL database requires SSL with custom CA certificate
# The CA bundle is a binary/DER format file that can't be stored as plain text
#
# Files:
# - config/certs/rds-ca-bundle.pem (multi-cert PEM bundle)
# - config/certs/corporate-ca.der (binary DER format)
#
# Usage: Mount in Django pod for database SSL connection
#
# Database connection with SSL:
# DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:pass@db-host:5432/dbname?sslmode=verify-full&sslrootcert=/certs/ca-bundle.pem

📄 CA Certificate Bundle Setup:

# Download AWS RDS CA bundle (for RDS PostgreSQL)
curl -o config/certs/rds-ca-bundle.pem \
  https://truststore.pki.rds.amazonaws.com/global/global-bundle.pem

# Verify certificate bundle
openssl verify -CAfile config/certs/rds-ca-bundle.pem config/certs/rds-ca-bundle.pem

# Check certificate count
grep -c "BEGIN CERTIFICATE" config/certs/rds-ca-bundle.pem

Command:

# Create ConfigMap from certificate file
kubectl create configmap db-ca-certs \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --from-file=ca-bundle.pem=config/certs/rds-ca-bundle.pem

# For binary files, use --from-file with binaryData
kubectl create configmap corporate-certs \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --from-file=corporate-ca.der=config/certs/corporate-ca.der

# Alternative: Create from multiple cert files
kubectl create configmap all-ca-certs \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --from-file=config/certs/

Output:

configmap/db-ca-certs created
configmap/corporate-certs created
configmap/all-ca-certs created

✅ Verification:

# Verify certificate content (PEM format)
kubectl get configmap db-ca-certs -n anihpj-production \
  -o jsonpath='{.data.ca-bundle\.pem}' | openssl x509 -noout -subject -issuer | head -4

# Check file size
kubectl get configmap db-ca-certs -n anihpj-production \
  -o jsonpath='{.data.ca-bundle\.pem}' | wc -c

# View deployment example with cert mount
cat <<'EOF'
# Deployment with CA certificate mount
spec:
  containers:
  - name: django
    env:
    - name: DATABASE_URL
      value: "postgres://user:pass@rds-instance:5432/jobpost_db?sslmode=verify-full&sslrootcert=/certs/ca-bundle.pem"
    volumeMounts:
    - name: db-certs
      mountPath: /certs
      readOnly: true
  volumes:
  - name: db-certs
    configMap:
      name: db-ca-certs
EOF
subject=C = US, O = Amazon, CN = Amazon RDS Root 2019 CA
issuer=C = US, O = Amazon, CN = Amazon RDS Root 2019 CA

182945

kubectl create cronjob

CREATE Scheduling Automation Batch

Create a cron job with the specified name

The kubectl create cronjob command creates a CronJob resource that runs Jobs on a repeating schedule defined by cron syntax. CronJobs are essential for automating periodic tasks in Kubernetes.

For the anihpj Django project, CronJobs handle:

  • Database cleanup - removing expired JobPost entries and orphaned records
  • Report generation - daily/weekly statistics for Skills usage and Author activity
  • Cache warming - pre-populating Location autocomplete caches
  • Email digests - sending daily job alerts to subscribed Users
  • Data synchronization - syncing JobPost data with external job boards
  • Backup operations - scheduled database and media file backups

Syntax

# Basic syntax
kubectl create cronjob NAME --image=IMAGE --schedule='CRON_EXPRESSION' -- [COMMAND] [args...]

# With restart policy
kubectl create cronjob NAME --image=IMAGE --schedule='*/5 * * * *' --restart=OnFailure -- command

# Dry-run to preview
kubectl create cronjob NAME --image=IMAGE --schedule='0 */6 * * *' --dry-run=client -o yaml -- command

Parameters

ParameterTypeDescription
--imagestringContainer image to run (required)
--schedulestringCron schedule expression (required)
--restartstringRestart policy: OnFailure, Never (default: OnFailure)
-n, --namespacestringTarget namespace
--dry-runstring"none", "server", or "client"
-o, --outputstringOutput format: json|yaml|name

Cron Schedule Reference

FieldRangeExampleDescription
Minute0-59*/15Every 15 minutes
Hour0-230,12Midnight and noon
Day of Month1-311First of month
Month1-12*/3Every 3 months
Day of Week0-61-5Monday to Friday

Common Schedule Patterns

ScheduleExpressionUse Case
Every minute* * * * *Health checks, monitoring
Every 5 minutes*/5 * * * *Cache refresh
Hourly0 * * * *Report generation
Daily at midnight0 0 * * *Cleanup jobs
Daily at 2 AM0 2 * * *Database backups
Weekly on Sunday0 0 * * 0Weekly reports
Monthly on 1st0 0 1 * *Monthly cleanup

Return Value

Exit CodeDescription
0CronJob created successfully
1Error (invalid schedule, image not found, already exists)

📁 Context: Django Management Commands for CronJobs

Architecture Pattern: Django management commands packaged in the same image as the web app, triggered by CronJobs.
Django Management Commands Structure
anihpj/
├── jobpost/
│   ├── models.py                      # Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost
│   └── management/
│       └── commands/
│           ├── __init__.py
│           ├── cleanup_expired_jobs.py   # Remove expired JobPosts
│           ├── generate_reports.py       # Daily statistics report
│           ├── send_job_alerts.py        # Email digest to Users
│           ├── sync_skills.py            # Sync skills from external API
│           ├── warm_cache.py             # Pre-populate Location caches
│           └── backup_database.py        # Database backup to S3
└── Dockerfile

Examples

Example 1: Create CronJob for Expired JobPost Cleanup

Scenario: Create a CronJob that runs daily at 2 AM to remove expired JobPost entries from the database.

📄 Context - Django Management Command (jobpost/management/commands/cleanup_expired_jobs.py):

# jobpost/management/commands/cleanup_expired_jobs.py
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from django.utils import timezone
from datetime import timedelta
from jobpost.models import JobPost
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Delete JobPosts that have expired based on expiry_date field'

    def add_arguments(self, parser):
        parser.add_argument(
            '--dry-run',
            action='store_true',
            help='Show what would be deleted without actually deleting',
        )
        parser.add_argument(
            '--days-old',
            type=int,
            default=30,
            help='Delete jobs expired more than N days ago (default: 30)',
        )

    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        dry_run = options['dry_run']
        days_old = options['days_old']
        cutoff_date = timezone.now() - timedelta(days=days_old)
        
        # Find expired JobPosts
        expired_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(
            expiry_date__lt=cutoff_date,
            is_active=False
        )
        
        count = expired_jobs.count()
        
        if dry_run:
            self.stdout.write(
                self.style.WARNING(f'DRY RUN: Would delete {count} expired JobPosts')
            )
            for job in expired_jobs[:10]:
                self.stdout.write(f'  - {job.title} (expired: {job.expiry_date})')
        else:
            # Get related data for logging
            authors_affected = expired_jobs.values('author').distinct().count()
            skills_to_cleanup = expired_jobs.values('skills').distinct().count()
            
            # Delete the jobs
            deleted_count, _ = expired_jobs.delete()
            
            logger.info(f'Deleted {deleted_count} expired JobPosts')
            self.stdout.write(
                self.style.SUCCESS(
                    f'Successfully deleted {deleted_count} expired JobPosts '
                    f'from {authors_affected} authors'
                )
            )

📄 Context - Dockerfile with Management Command Support:

# Dockerfile for anihpj Django app with management commands
FROM python:3.11-slim

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

WORKDIR /app

# Install dependencies
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy application code including management commands
COPY anihpj/ ./anihpj/
COPY jobpost/ ./jobpost/
COPY manage.py .

# Create non-root user for security
RUN useradd -m -u 1000 appuser && chown -R appuser:appuser /app
USER appuser

# Default command for web server
CMD ["gunicorn", "anihpj.wsgi:application", "-b", "0.0.0.0:8000"]

# Management commands can be run via:
# docker run myimage python manage.py cleanup_expired_jobs

Command:

# Create CronJob for daily cleanup at 2 AM
kubectl create cronjob jobpost-cleanup \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --image=registry.example.com/anihpj:latest \
  --schedule='0 2 * * *' \
  --restart=OnFailure \
  -- python manage.py cleanup_expired_jobs --days-old=30

Output:

cronjob.batch/jobpost-cleanup created

✅ Verification:

# View the created CronJob
kubectl get cronjob jobpost-cleanup -n anihpj-production

# Check detailed configuration
kubectl describe cronjob jobpost-cleanup -n anihpj-production

# Manually trigger a job to test
kubectl create job --from=cronjob/jobpost-cleanup jobpost-cleanup-manual -n anihpj-production

# Watch job execution
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-production -w

# View logs from completed job
kubectl logs job/jobpost-cleanup-manual -n anihpj-production
NAME              SCHEDULE    SUSPEND   ACTIVE   LAST SCHEDULE   AGE
jobpost-cleanup   0 2 * * *   False     0        <none>          10s

job.batch/jobpost-cleanup-manual created

NAME                      COMPLETIONS   DURATION   AGE
jobpost-cleanup-manual    1/1           15s        20s

Successfully deleted 47 expired JobPosts from 12 authors
Example 2: Create CronJob for Daily Statistics Report

Scenario: Create a CronJob that generates daily statistics about JobPost activity, Skills usage, and Author metrics.

📄 Context - Report Generation Command (jobpost/management/commands/generate_reports.py):

# jobpost/management/commands/generate_reports.py
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from django.utils import timezone
from django.db.models import Count, Avg
from datetime import timedelta
from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, User, Author, Location
import json
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Generate daily statistics report for anihpj jobpost platform'

    def add_arguments(self, parser):
        parser.add_argument(
            '--output',
            type=str,
            default='stdout',
            help='Output destination: stdout, s3, or file path',
        )
        parser.add_argument(
            '--period',
            type=str,
            default='daily',
            choices=['daily', 'weekly', 'monthly'],
            help='Report period',
        )

    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        period = options['period']
        now = timezone.now()
        
        if period == 'daily':
            start_date = now - timedelta(days=1)
        elif period == 'weekly':
            start_date = now - timedelta(weeks=1)
        else:
            start_date = now - timedelta(days=30)
        
        # Generate statistics
        report = {
            'generated_at': now.isoformat(),
            'period': period,
            'start_date': start_date.isoformat(),
            'end_date': now.isoformat(),
            'jobposts': {
                'total': JobPost.objects.count(),
                'new': JobPost.objects.filter(created_at__gte=start_date).count(),
                'active': JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).count(),
                'views_avg': JobPost.objects.aggregate(Avg('view_count'))['view_count__avg'],
            },
            'skills': {
                'total': Skills.objects.count(),
                'top_skills': list(Skills.objects.annotate(
                    num_jobs=Count('jobpost')
                ).order_by('-num_jobs')[:10].values('name', 'num_jobs')),
            },
            'authors': {
                'total': Author.objects.count(),
                'active': Author.objects.filter(
                    jobpost__created_at__gte=start_date
                ).distinct().count(),
                'avg_jobs_per_author': JobPost.objects.values('author').annotate(
                    count=Count('id')
                ).aggregate(Avg('count'))['count__avg'],
            },
            'users': {
                'total': User.objects.count(),
                'new': User.objects.filter(date_joined__gte=start_date).count(),
            },
            'locations': {
                'total': Location.objects.count(),
                'top_locations': list(Location.objects.annotate(
                    num_jobs=Count('jobpost')
                ).order_by('-num_jobs')[:5].values('city', 'country', 'num_jobs')),
            },
        }
        
        # Output report
        report_json = json.dumps(report, indent=2, default=str)
        
        if options['output'] == 'stdout':
            self.stdout.write(report_json)
        else:
            logger.info(f"Report generated: {report['jobposts']['new']} new jobs")
        
        self.stdout.write(
            self.style.SUCCESS(f'Report generated successfully for {period} period')
        )

Command:

# Create CronJob for hourly reports at the top of each hour
kubectl create cronjob jobpost-reports \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --image=registry.example.com/anihpj:latest \
  --schedule='0 * * * *' \
  --restart=OnFailure \
  -- python manage.py generate_reports --period=daily --output=s3

Output:

cronjob.batch/jobpost-reports created

✅ Verification:

# Generate YAML manifest for GitOps

# Test the report generation
kubectl create job --from=cronjob/jobpost-reports test-report -n anihpj-production
kubectl logs job/test-report -n anihpj-production -f
NAME              SCHEDULE     SUSPEND   ACTIVE   LAST SCHEDULE   AGE
jobpost-cleanup   0 2 * * *    False     0        2h              3h
jobpost-reports   0 * * * *    False     0        <none>          5s

{
  "generated_at": "2024-01-15T10:00:00.000Z",
  "period": "daily",
  "jobposts": {
    "total": 1247,
    "new": 23,
    "active": 892
  },
  "skills": {
    "top_skills": [
      {"name": "Python", "num_jobs": 342},
      {"name": "Kubernetes", "num_jobs": 256}
    ]
  }
}
Report generated successfully for daily period
Example 3: Create CronJob for Email Digest to Subscribed Users

Scenario: Create a CronJob that runs every morning at 8 AM to send personalized job alert emails to subscribed Users.

📄 Context - Email Alert Command (jobpost/management/commands/send_job_alerts.py):

# jobpost/management/commands/send_job_alerts.py
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from django.core.mail import send_mass_mail
from django.template.loader import render_to_string
from django.utils import timezone
from datetime import timedelta
from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Send personalized job alert emails to subscribed users'

    def add_arguments(self, parser):
        parser.add_argument(
            '--dry-run',
            action='store_true',
            help='Print emails without sending',
        )
        parser.add_argument(
            '--batch-size',
            type=int,
            default=100,
            help='Number of emails per batch',
        )

    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        dry_run = options['dry_run']
        batch_size = options['batch_size']
        yesterday = timezone.now() - timedelta(days=1)
        
        # Get subscribed users with skill preferences
        subscribed_users = User.objects.filter(
            is_subscribed_alerts=True,
            preferred_skills__isnull=False
        ).prefetch_related('preferred_skills').distinct()
        
        emails_to_send = []
        
        for user in subscribed_users:
            # Find new jobs matching user's preferred skills
            user_skills = user.preferred_skills.all()
            matching_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(
                created_at__gte=yesterday,
                skills__in=user_skills,
                is_active=True
            ).distinct()[:10]
            
            if matching_jobs.exists():
                # Render email template
                context = {
                    'user': user,
                    'jobs': matching_jobs,
                    'skill_names': ', '.join([s.name for s in user_skills[:3]]),
                }
                
                subject = f'New Job Opportunities in {context["skill_names"]}'
                message = render_to_string('emails/job_alert.txt', context)
                html_message = render_to_string('emails/job_alert.html', context)
                
                emails_to_send.append((
                    subject,
                    message,
                    'alerts@jobpost.example.com',
                    [user.email],
                ))
        
        total_emails = len(emails_to_send)
        
        if dry_run:
            self.stdout.write(
                self.style.WARNING(f'DRY RUN: Would send {total_emails} emails')
            )
            for email in emails_to_send[:5]:
                self.stdout.write(f'  To: {email[3][0]} | Subject: {email[0]}')
        else:
            # Send in batches
            sent_count = 0
            for i in range(0, total_emails, batch_size):
                batch = emails_to_send[i:i+batch_size]
                send_mass_mail(batch, fail_silently=False)
                sent_count += len(batch)
                logger.info(f'Sent batch {i//batch_size + 1}: {len(batch)} emails')
            
            self.stdout.write(
                self.style.SUCCESS(f'Successfully sent {sent_count} job alert emails')
            )

📄 Context - Complete CronJob Manifest with Environment:

# k8s/cronjobs/send-job-alerts.yaml
# Full CronJob manifest showing ConfigMap and Secret integration
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: send-job-alerts
  namespace: anihpj-production
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: cronjob
    function: email-alerts
spec:
  schedule: "0 8 * * *"  # Daily at 8 AM
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
          containers:
          - name: send-alerts
            image: registry.example.com/anihpj:latest
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "send_job_alerts"]
            envFrom:
            - configMapRef:
                name: django-config
            - secretRef:
                name: django-secrets
            env:
            - name: EMAIL_HOST
              valueFrom:
                configMapKeyRef:
                  name: email-config
                  key: SMTP_HOST
            resources:
              requests:
                memory: "256Mi"
                cpu: "100m"
              limits:
                memory: "512Mi"
                cpu: "500m"

Command:

# Create basic CronJob
kubectl create cronjob send-job-alerts \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --image=registry.example.com/anihpj:latest \
  --schedule='0 8 * * *' \
  --restart=OnFailure \
  -- python manage.py send_job_alerts --batch-size=50

# Generate full YAML for GitOps customization
kubectl create cronjob send-job-alerts \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --image=registry.example.com/anihpj:latest \
  --schedule='0 8 * * *' \
  --restart=OnFailure \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml \
  -- python manage.py send_job_alerts > cronjob-alerts.yaml

Output:

cronjob.batch/send-job-alerts created

✅ Verification:

# Verify schedule is correct
kubectl get cronjob send-job-alerts -n anihpj-production -o jsonpath='{.spec.schedule}'

# Check next scheduled run
kubectl get cronjob send-job-alerts -n anihpj-production -o jsonpath='{.status.lastScheduleTime}'

# Run test with dry-run flag
kubectl create job test-alerts --from=cronjob/send-job-alerts -n anihpj-production
kubectl exec -it job/test-alerts -n anihpj-production -- python manage.py send_job_alerts --dry-run
0 8 * * *

DRY RUN: Would send 156 emails
  To: john@example.com | Subject: New Job Opportunities in Python, Django
  To: jane@example.com | Subject: New Job Opportunities in Kubernetes, AWS
  To: bob@example.com | Subject: New Job Opportunities in React, TypeScript
Example 4: Create CronJob for Database Backup

Scenario: Create a CronJob that performs daily PostgreSQL database backups at 3 AM and uploads them to cloud storage.

📄 Context - Backup Script and Infrastructure:

# k8s/cronjobs/database-backup/
# Infrastructure for database backup CronJob
#
# Components:
# 1. PostgreSQL client container (pg_dump)
# 2. Cloud storage credentials (S3/GCS)
# 3. Backup retention policy
# 4. Alerting on failure

# backup-script ConfigMap
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: backup-script
  namespace: anihpj-production
data:
  backup.sh: |
    #!/bin/bash
    set -e
    
    # Configuration from environment
    TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
    BACKUP_FILE="jobpost-backup-${TIMESTAMP}.sql.gz"
    S3_BUCKET="${S3_BACKUP_BUCKET:-anihpj-backups}"
    RETENTION_DAYS="${BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS:-30}"
    
    echo "Starting backup: ${BACKUP_FILE}"
    
    # Perform PostgreSQL dump
    pg_dump \
      -h "${DB_HOST}" \
      -U "${DB_USER}" \
      -d "${DB_NAME}" \
      --format=plain \
      --no-owner \
      --no-acl | gzip > "/tmp/${BACKUP_FILE}"
    
    # Upload to S3
    aws s3 cp "/tmp/${BACKUP_FILE}" "s3://${S3_BUCKET}/daily/${BACKUP_FILE}"
    
    # Clean up old backups
    aws s3 ls "s3://${S3_BUCKET}/daily/" | \
      awk -v date="$(date -d "-${RETENTION_DAYS} days" +%Y%m%d)" \
      '$4 ~ /jobpost-backup-/ && substr($4,16,8) < date {print $4}' | \
      xargs -I {} aws s3 rm "s3://${S3_BUCKET}/daily/{}"
    
    echo "Backup completed: ${BACKUP_FILE}"
    
    # Verify backup exists
    aws s3 ls "s3://${S3_BUCKET}/daily/${BACKUP_FILE}"

📄 Complete CronJob Manifest:

# k8s/cronjobs/database-backup.yaml
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: database-backup
  namespace: anihpj-production
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: backup
spec:
  schedule: "0 3 * * *"  # Daily at 3 AM
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 7
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 3
      template:
        spec:
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
          serviceAccountName: backup-sa
          containers:
          - name: backup
            image: postgres:15-alpine
            command: ["/bin/sh", "/scripts/backup.sh"]
            env:
            - name: DB_HOST
              valueFrom:
                configMapKeyRef:
                  name: django-config
                  key: DB_HOST
            - name: DB_NAME
              valueFrom:
                configMapKeyRef:
                  name: django-config
                  key: DB_NAME
            - name: DB_USER
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: django-secrets
                  key: DB_USER
            - name: PGPASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: django-secrets
                  key: DB_PASSWORD
            - name: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: aws-backup-credentials
                  key: access-key-id
            - name: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: aws-backup-credentials
                  key: secret-access-key
            - name: S3_BACKUP_BUCKET
              value: "anihpj-production-backups"
            - name: BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS
              value: "30"
            volumeMounts:
            - name: backup-script
              mountPath: /scripts
            resources:
              requests:
                memory: "256Mi"
                cpu: "100m"
              limits:
                memory: "1Gi"
                cpu: "500m"
          volumes:
          - name: backup-script
            configMap:
              name: backup-script
              defaultMode: 0755

Command:

# Create simple CronJob (for testing with pre-built backup image)
kubectl create cronjob database-backup \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --image=postgres:15-alpine \
  --schedule='0 3 * * *' \
  --restart=OnFailure \
  -- sh -c 'pg_dump -h $DB_HOST -U $DB_USER $DB_NAME | gzip > /backup/db-$(date +%Y%m%d).sql.gz'

# For production, apply the full manifest
kubectl apply -f k8s/cronjobs/database-backup.yaml

Output:

cronjob.batch/database-backup created

✅ Verification:

# Trigger manual backup
kubectl create job manual-backup --from=cronjob/database-backup -n anihpj-production

# Monitor backup progress
kubectl logs -f job/manual-backup -n anihpj-production

# Check backup in S3
aws s3 ls s3://anihpj-production-backups/daily/ --recursive | tail -5

# Verify backup integrity (download and test)
aws s3 cp s3://anihpj-production-backups/daily/jobpost-backup-20240115-030000.sql.gz /tmp/
gunzip -t /tmp/jobpost-backup-20240115-030000.sql.gz && echo "Backup is valid"
Starting backup: jobpost-backup-20240115-030000.sql.gz
Backup completed: jobpost-backup-20240115-030000.sql.gz
2024-01-15 03:00:05      15728640 daily/jobpost-backup-20240115-030000.sql.gz

2024-01-14 03:00:05 15234567 daily/jobpost-backup-20240114-030000.sql.gz
2024-01-15 03:00:05 15728640 daily/jobpost-backup-20240115-030000.sql.gz

Backup is valid
Example 5: Create CronJob for Skills Data Synchronization

Scenario: Create a CronJob that synchronizes the Skills database with an external skills taxonomy API every 6 hours.

📄 Context - Skills Sync Command (jobpost/management/commands/sync_skills.py):

# jobpost/management/commands/sync_skills.py
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from django.db import transaction
from jobpost.models import Skills
import requests
import logging
import os

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Synchronize Skills model with external skills taxonomy API'

    def add_arguments(self, parser):
        parser.add_argument(
            '--api-url',
            type=str,
            default=os.environ.get('SKILLS_API_URL', 'https://api.skills.io/v1/skills'),
            help='Skills taxonomy API endpoint',
        )
        parser.add_argument(
            '--batch-size',
            type=int,
            default=100,
            help='Number of skills to process per batch',
        )
        parser.add_argument(
            '--dry-run',
            action='store_true',
            help='Show changes without applying',
        )

    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        api_url = options['api_url']
        batch_size = options['batch_size']
        dry_run = options['dry_run']
        
        self.stdout.write(f'Fetching skills from {api_url}')
        
        try:
            # Fetch skills from external API
            response = requests.get(
                api_url,
                headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {os.environ.get("SKILLS_API_KEY")}'},
                timeout=30
            )
            response.raise_for_status()
            external_skills = response.json()['skills']
            
        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f'Failed to fetch skills: {e}')
            raise
        
        stats = {'created': 0, 'updated': 0, 'unchanged': 0}
        
        with transaction.atomic():
            for skill_data in external_skills:
                skill, created = Skills.objects.update_or_create(
                    external_id=skill_data['id'],
                    defaults={
                        'name': skill_data['name'],
                        'category': skill_data.get('category', 'General'),
                        'synonyms': skill_data.get('synonyms', []),
                        'is_active': skill_data.get('status') == 'active',
                    }
                )
                
                if created:
                    stats['created'] += 1
                    if not dry_run:
                        logger.info(f'Created skill: {skill.name}')
                elif skill.name != skill_data['name']:
                    stats['updated'] += 1
                else:
                    stats['unchanged'] += 1
            
            if dry_run:
                raise Exception('DRY RUN - Rolling back transaction')
        
        self.stdout.write(
            self.style.SUCCESS(
                f'Sync complete: {stats["created"]} created, '
                f'{stats["updated"]} updated, {stats["unchanged"]} unchanged'
            )
        )

Command:

# Create CronJob for skills sync every 6 hours
kubectl create cronjob skills-sync \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --image=registry.example.com/anihpj:latest \
  --schedule='0 */6 * * *' \
  --restart=OnFailure \
  -- python manage.py sync_skills --batch-size=100

Output:

cronjob.batch/skills-sync created

✅ Verification:

# List all job-related CronJobs
kubectl get cronjobs -n anihpj-production -l app=anihpj

# Test sync with dry-run
kubectl create job skills-sync-test --from=cronjob/skills-sync -n anihpj-production
kubectl exec job/skills-sync-test -n anihpj-production -- \
  python manage.py sync_skills --dry-run

# Monitor sync logs
kubectl logs -f job/skills-sync-test -n anihpj-production
NAME              SCHEDULE       SUSPEND   ACTIVE   LAST SCHEDULE   AGE
jobpost-cleanup   0 2 * * *      False     0        12h             2d
jobpost-reports   0 * * * *      False     0        45m             1d
send-job-alerts   0 8 * * *      False     0        3h              1d
database-backup   0 3 * * *      False     0        9h              2d
skills-sync       0 */6 * * *    False     0        <none>          5s

Fetching skills from https://api.skills.io/v1/skills
DRY RUN - Rolling back transaction
Sync complete: 45 created, 12 updated, 2890 unchanged
Example 6: Create CronJob with Concurrency and History Policies

Scenario: Create a CronJob with specific concurrency handling, job history limits, and deadline settings for cache warming operations.

📄 Context - Cache Warming Strategy for Location Autocomplete:

# jobpost/management/commands/warm_cache.py
# Preload Location autocomplete data into Redis cache
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from django.core.cache import cache
from jobpost.models import Location, JobPost
from django.db.models import Count
import json
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Warm Location autocomplete cache for faster search experience'

    def add_arguments(self, parser):
        parser.add_argument(
            '--prefix-length',
            type=int,
            default=3,
            help='Minimum prefix length for autocomplete',
        )
        parser.add_argument(
            '--cache-timeout',
            type=int,
            default=3600,
            help='Cache timeout in seconds',
        )

    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        prefix_length = options['prefix_length']
        timeout = options['cache_timeout']
        
        # Get locations with job counts
        locations = Location.objects.annotate(
            job_count=Count('jobpost')
        ).filter(job_count__gt=0).order_by('-job_count')
        
        prefixes_cached = set()
        
        for loc in locations:
            city_lower = loc.city.lower()
            
            # Generate all prefixes from min length to full name
            for i in range(prefix_length, len(city_lower) + 1):
                prefix = city_lower[:i]
                
                if prefix not in prefixes_cached:
                    # Get all matching locations for this prefix
                    matches = Location.objects.filter(
                        city__istartswith=prefix
                    ).annotate(
                        job_count=Count('jobpost')
                    ).order_by('-job_count')[:10]
                    
                    cache_data = [
                        {
                            'id': m.id,
                            'city': m.city,
                            'country': m.country,
                            'job_count': m.job_count,
                        }
                        for m in matches
                    ]
                    
                    cache_key = f'location:autocomplete:{prefix}'
                    cache.set(cache_key, json.dumps(cache_data), timeout)
                    prefixes_cached.add(prefix)
        
        self.stdout.write(
            self.style.SUCCESS(
                f'Cached {len(prefixes_cached)} location prefixes'
            )
        )

📄 Full CronJob with Advanced Settings:

# k8s/cronjobs/cache-warmer.yaml
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: cache-warmer
  namespace: anihpj-production
  annotations:
    description: "Warm Location autocomplete cache every 15 minutes"
spec:
  schedule: "*/15 * * * *"
  # Don't allow concurrent runs - skip if previous still running
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  # Keep last 5 successful jobs for debugging
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 5
  # Keep last 3 failed jobs for troubleshooting
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  # Job must start within 100 seconds of scheduled time
  startingDeadlineSeconds: 100
  # Suspend scheduling (useful for maintenance)
  suspend: false
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      # Don't retry more than 2 times
      backoffLimit: 2
      # Job timeout - kill if running longer than 5 minutes
      activeDeadlineSeconds: 300
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: cache-warmer
        spec:
          restartPolicy: Never
          containers:
          - name: cache-warmer
            image: registry.example.com/anihpj:latest
            command:
            - python
            - manage.py
            - warm_cache
            - --prefix-length=2
            - --cache-timeout=900
            envFrom:
            - configMapRef:
                name: django-config
            - secretRef:
                name: django-secrets
            env:
            - name: REDIS_URL
              valueFrom:
                configMapKeyRef:
                  name: redis-config
                  key: REDIS_URL
            resources:
              requests:
                memory: "128Mi"
                cpu: "50m"
              limits:
                memory: "256Mi"
                cpu: "200m"

Command:

# Create CronJob and generate YAML for customization
kubectl create cronjob cache-warmer \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --image=registry.example.com/anihpj:latest \
  --schedule='*/15 * * * *' \
  --restart=Never \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml \
  -- python manage.py warm_cache > cache-warmer-base.yaml

# Apply full manifest with advanced settings
kubectl apply -f k8s/cronjobs/cache-warmer.yaml

Output:

cronjob.batch/cache-warmer created

✅ Verification:

# View CronJob settings
kubectl get cronjob cache-warmer -n anihpj-production \
  -o jsonpath='{.spec.concurrencyPolicy}' && echo
kubectl get cronjob cache-warmer -n anihpj-production \
  -o jsonpath='{.spec.successfulJobsHistoryLimit}' && echo

# List recent job history
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-production -l app=anihpj,component=cache-warmer

# Suspend CronJob during maintenance
kubectl patch cronjob cache-warmer -n anihpj-production -p '{"spec":{"suspend":true}}'

# Resume CronJob
kubectl patch cronjob cache-warmer -n anihpj-production -p '{"spec":{"suspend":false}}'

# Delete completed jobs to clean up
kubectl delete jobs -n anihpj-production -l app=anihpj,component=cache-warmer
Forbid
5

NAME                         COMPLETIONS   DURATION   AGE
cache-warmer-28431234        1/1           23s        15m
cache-warmer-28431235        1/1           21s        0s

cronjob.batch/cache-warmer patched
cronjob.batch/cache-warmer patched
job.batch "cache-warmer-28431234" deleted

kubectl create deployment

CREATE Workload Pods ReplicaSets

Create a deployment with the specified name

The kubectl create deployment command creates a Deployment resource that manages a replicated application. Deployments provide declarative updates for Pods and ReplicaSets, enabling zero-downtime rolling updates and easy rollbacks.

For the anihpj Django project, Deployments are the primary workload resource for:

  • jobpost-web - Django/Gunicorn web servers serving the jobpost API
  • jobpost-worker - Celery workers processing async tasks for JobPost operations
  • jobpost-scheduler - Celery beat scheduler for periodic tasks
  • nginx - Reverse proxy serving static files and routing to Gunicorn
  • redis - Cache backend for Skills/Location autocomplete

Syntax

# Basic deployment
kubectl create deployment NAME --image=IMAGE

# With replicas
kubectl create deployment NAME --image=IMAGE --replicas=3

# With port specification
kubectl create deployment NAME --image=IMAGE --port=8000

# Dry-run for YAML generation
kubectl create deployment NAME --image=IMAGE --replicas=3 --port=8000 --dry-run=client -o yaml

Parameters

ParameterTypeDescription
--imagestring[]Container image(s) to run (required)
--replicasintNumber of replicas (default: 1)
--portintContainer port to expose
-n, --namespacestringTarget namespace
--dry-runstring"none", "server", or "client"
-o, --outputstringOutput format: json|yaml|name
--save-configboolSave configuration in annotation
--validatestringValidation mode

Deployment Update Strategies

StrategyDescriptionUse Case
RollingUpdateGradually replaces old pods with new onesDefault - zero downtime updates
RecreateTerminates all pods before creating new onesDatabase migrations, incompatible versions

Return Value

Exit CodeDescription
0Deployment created successfully
1Error (invalid image, already exists, validation failure)

📁 Context: Django Deployment Architecture for anihpj

Production Architecture: Multi-tier deployment with web servers, workers, and supporting services.
Deployment Architecture Diagram
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         anihpj Production Namespace                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│  ┌────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────────┐     ┌────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Ingress       │────▶│  nginx Deployment    │────▶│  jobpost-web       │ │
│  │  (TLS/Routing) │     │  (3 replicas)        │     │  Deployment        │ │
│  └────────────────┘     │  - Static files      │     │  (5 replicas)      │ │
│                         │  - Reverse proxy     │     │  - Gunicorn        │ │
│                         └──────────────────────┘     │  - Django app      │ │
│                                                      └─────────┬──────────┘ │
│                                                                │             │
│                         ┌──────────────────────┐              │             │
│                         │  redis Deployment    │◀─────────────┘             │
│                         │  (Cache/Sessions)    │                            │
│                         └──────────────────────┘                            │
│                                    │                                        │
│                         ┌──────────▼───────────┐     ┌────────────────────┐ │
│                         │  jobpost-worker      │     │  PostgreSQL        │ │
│                         │  Deployment          │────▶│  (StatefulSet)     │ │
│                         │  (3 replicas)        │     │  - Skills, User    │ │
│                         │  - Celery workers    │     │  - Author, Location│ │
│                         └──────────────────────┘     │  - JobPost models  │ │
│                                                      └────────────────────┘ │
│                         ┌──────────────────────┐                            │
│                         │  jobpost-scheduler   │                            │
│                         │  Deployment          │                            │
│                         │  (1 replica)         │                            │
│                         │  - Celery beat       │                            │
│                         └──────────────────────┘                            │
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Examples

Example 1: Create Basic Django Web Deployment

Scenario: Create the main Django web deployment running Gunicorn to serve the jobpost API.

📄 Context - Production Dockerfile:

# Dockerfile for anihpj Django production deployment
FROM python:3.11-slim as builder

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    build-essential \
    libpq-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Install Python dependencies
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip wheel --no-cache-dir --no-deps --wheel-dir /wheels -r requirements.txt

# Production image
FROM python:3.11-slim

# Install runtime dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    libpq5 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Copy wheels and install
COPY --from=builder /wheels /wheels
RUN pip install --no-cache /wheels/*

# Copy application
COPY anihpj/ ./anihpj/
COPY jobpost/ ./jobpost/
COPY manage.py .

# Create appuser
RUN useradd -m -u 1000 appuser
RUN mkdir -p /app/staticfiles /app/media && \
    chown -R appuser:appuser /app

# Collect static files
RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput

USER appuser

EXPOSE 8000

# Gunicorn with optimal settings for Kubernetes
CMD ["gunicorn", "anihpj.wsgi:application", \
     "--bind", "0.0.0.0:8000", \
     "--workers", "4", \
     "--worker-class", "sync", \
     "--worker-tmp-dir", "/dev/shm", \
     "--access-logfile", "-", \
     "--error-logfile", "-", \
     "--capture-output", \
     "--enable-stdio-inheritance"]

Command:

# Create basic deployment
kubectl create deployment jobpost-web \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --image=registry.example.com/anihpj:v1.0.0 \
  --replicas=3 \
  --port=8000

# Add labels after creation
kubectl label deployment jobpost-web \
  app=anihpj \
  component=web \
  tier=frontend \
  -n anihpj-production

Output:

deployment.apps/jobpost-web created
deployment.apps/jobpost-web labeled

✅ Verification:

# Check deployment status
kubectl get deployment jobpost-web -n anihpj-production

# Watch pods come up
kubectl get pods -l app=anihpj,component=web -n anihpj-production -w

# Check rollout status
kubectl rollout status deployment/jobpost-web -n anihpj-production

# View deployment details
kubectl describe deployment jobpost-web -n anihpj-production | head -30
NAME          READY   UP-TO-DATE   AVAILABLE   AGE
jobpost-web   3/3     3            3           45s

NAME                           READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
jobpost-web-7d9f8c6b5-abc12    1/1     Running   0          30s
jobpost-web-7d9f8c6b5-def34    1/1     Running   0          30s
jobpost-web-7d9f8c6b5-ghi56    1/1     Running   0          30s

deployment "jobpost-web" successfully rolled out

Name:                   jobpost-web
Namespace:              anihpj-production
CreationTimestamp:      Mon, 15 Jan 2024 10:00:00 +0000
Labels:                 app=anihpj
                        component=web
                        tier=frontend
Selector:               app=jobpost-web
Replicas:               3 desired | 3 updated | 3 total | 3 available | 0 unavailable
Example 2: Create Multi-Container Deployment with Sidecar

Scenario: Create a deployment with the main Django container and a logging sidecar container for structured log shipping.

📄 Context - Sidecar Pattern for Log Aggregation:

# k8s/deployments/jobpost-web-with-sidecar.yaml
# Pattern: Django app + Fluent Bit sidecar for structured logging
#
# Main container writes logs to shared volume
# Sidecar reads logs and ships to Elasticsearch/CloudWatch
#
# Benefits:
# - Decoupled logging from application
# - Consistent log format across all containers
# - Easy to swap log destinations

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: jobpost-web
  namespace: anihpj-production
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: web
    spec:
      containers:
      # Main Django/Gunicorn container
      - name: django
        image: registry.example.com/anihpj:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        volumeMounts:
        - name: logs
          mountPath: /var/log/django
        env:
        - name: LOG_FILE
          value: /var/log/django/app.log
      
      # Fluent Bit sidecar for log shipping
      - name: fluent-bit
        image: fluent/fluent-bit:2.1
        volumeMounts:
        - name: logs
          mountPath: /var/log/django
          readOnly: true
        - name: fluent-bit-config
          mountPath: /fluent-bit/etc/
      
      volumes:
      - name: logs
        emptyDir: {}
      - name: fluent-bit-config
        configMap:
          name: fluent-bit-config

Command:

# Create deployment with multiple images (becomes multi-container pod)
kubectl create deployment jobpost-web \
  --namespace=anihpj-production \
  --image=registry.example.com/anihpj:v1.0.0 \
  --image=fluent/fluent-bit:2.1 \
  --replicas=3 \
  --port=8000 \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml > jobpost-web-deployment.yaml

# Edit to add volume mounts and configuration
# Then apply
kubectl apply -f jobpost-web-deployment.yaml

Output:

# Generated base YAML
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  labels:
    app: jobpost-web
  name: jobpost-web
  namespace: anihpj-production
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: jobpost-web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: jobpost-web
    spec:
      containers:
      - image: registry.example.com/anihpj:v1.0.0
        name: anihpj
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
      - image: fluent/fluent-bit:2.1
        name: fluent-bit

✅ Verification:

# Check containers in pods
kubectl get pods -l app=jobpost-web -n anihpj-production -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[*].name}'

# Verify both containers are running
kubectl get pods -l app=jobpost-web -n anihpj-production

# Check logs from specific container
kubectl logs deployment/jobpost-web -c django -n anihpj-production --tail=20
kubectl logs deployment/jobpost-web -c fluent-bit -n anihpj-production --tail=20
anihpj fluent-bit

NAME                           READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
jobpost-web-5d8f9c7b6-abc12    2/2     Running   0          2m
jobpost-web-5d8f9c7b6-def34    2/2     Running   0          2m
jobpost-web-5d8f9c7b6-ghi56    2/2     Running   0          2m

[2024-01-15 10:02:15 +0000] [1] [INFO] Starting gunicorn 21.2.0
[2024-01-15 10:02:15 +0000] [1] [INFO] Listening at: http://0.0.0.0:8000 (1)
