The kubectl version command displays kubectl client version and Kubernetes server version. Important for ensuring version compatibility and debugging issues.

Syntax

kubectl version [flags]

Parameters

  • --client: Show only client version
  • --short: Brief version format (deprecated)
  • -o, --output: Output format (yaml, json)

Return Value

Client and server version details.

Examples

Example 1: Check Full Version

Display complete version information.

Command:

kubectl version

Output:

Client Version: v1.31.0
Kustomize Version: v5.4.2
Server Version: v1.30.2
Example 2: Client Version Only

Check client version without server connection.

Command:

kubectl version --client

Output:

Client Version: v1.31.0
Kustomize Version: v5.4.2
Example 3: JSON Output for Scripting

Get version info as JSON for parsing.

Command:

kubectl version -o json | jq .

Output:

{
  "clientVersion": {
    "major": "1",
    "minor": "31",
    "gitVersion": "v1.31.0",
    "gitCommit": "9edcffcde5595e8a5b1a35f88c421764e575afce",
    "gitTreeState": "clean",
    "buildDate": "2024-08-13T07:36:11Z",
    "goVersion": "go1.22.5",
    "compiler": "gc",
    "platform": "linux/amd64"
  },
  "serverVersion": {
    "major": "1",
    "minor": "30",
    "gitVersion": "v1.30.2",
    "gitCommit": "39683505b630ff2121012f3c5b16215a1449d5ed",
    "gitTreeState": "clean",
    "buildDate": "2024-06-11T20:22:13Z",
    "goVersion": "go1.22.4",
    "compiler": "gc",
    "platform": "linux/amd64"
  }
}
Example 4: Version Compatibility Check

Script to check version compatibility.

Context - Compatibility Check Script:

#!/bin/bash
# scripts/check-version-compat.sh

echo "🔍 Kubernetes Version Compatibility Check"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""

# Get versions
CLIENT_VERSION=$(kubectl version --client -o json | jq -r '.clientVersion.gitVersion')
SERVER_VERSION=$(kubectl version -o json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.serverVersion.gitVersion')

echo "Client: $CLIENT_VERSION"
echo "Server: $SERVER_VERSION"
echo ""

# Extract minor versions
CLIENT_MINOR=$(echo "$CLIENT_VERSION" | sed 's/v[0-9]*\.\([0-9]*\)\..*/\1/')
SERVER_MINOR=$(echo "$SERVER_VERSION" | sed 's/v[0-9]*\.\([0-9]*\)\..*/\1/')

# Check version skew (should be within 1 minor version)
DIFF=$((CLIENT_MINOR - SERVER_MINOR))
if [ ${DIFF#-} -le 1 ]; then
    echo "✅ Version compatibility: OK"
    echo "   Client and server are within 1 minor version"
else
    echo "⚠️  Version compatibility: WARNING"
    echo "   Client and server differ by more than 1 minor version"
    echo "   This may cause compatibility issues"
fi

Command:

./scripts/check-version-compat.sh

Output:

🔍 Kubernetes Version Compatibility Check
==========================================

Client: v1.31.0
Server: v1.30.2

✅ Version compatibility: OK
   Client and server are within 1 minor version
Example 5: CI/CD Version Verification

Verify versions in deployment pipeline.

Context - GitHub Actions Workflow:

# .github/workflows/deploy.yml
name: Deploy with Version Check

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup kubectl
        uses: azure/setup-kubectl@v3
        with:
          version: 'v1.30.0'  # Match cluster version
          
      - name: Configure kubectl
        run: |
          echo "${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG }}" | base64 -d > ~/.kube/config
          
      - name: Verify versions
        run: |
          echo "📋 Version Information"
          kubectl version
          
          # Extract versions for comparison
          CLIENT=$(kubectl version --client -o json | jq -r '.clientVersion.minor')
          SERVER=$(kubectl version -o json | jq -r '.serverVersion.minor')
          
          # Check compatibility
          DIFF=$((CLIENT - SERVER))
          if [ ${DIFF#-} -gt 1 ]; then
            echo "::error::kubectl client version ($CLIENT) too far from server ($SERVER)"
            exit 1
          fi
          
      - name: Deploy
        run: |
          kubectl apply -f k8s/
          kubectl rollout status deployment/django-web

📝 CREATE SUBCOMMANDS

Specialized create commands for creating specific Kubernetes resources. Includes commands for creating deployments, services, secrets, configmaps, and more.
